title: dysoxylum papuanum
intro: Dysoxylum papuanum, also known as the Papuan dysoxylum or Papuan lancewood, is a plant species native to Papua New Guinea and is part of the family Meliaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its chemical compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Dysoxylum papuanum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections, making it a valuable natural remedy in traditional medicine. Dysoxylum papuanum can be prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal use, and its bark and leaves are often used in herbal preparations.
